rulings.Case Title: JUAN BONILLA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 30, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Dismiss Denied. The five-year period of CCP § 583.310 is suspended during the period of time the case remains submitted to arbitration during the last six months prior to the expiration of the five-year statute, even though the case never had an arbitration hearing. (Niesner v. Kusch (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 291, 293.) On November 30, 2007, Plaintiff filed his Complaint. Applying the language of CCP 1141.17, four years and six months after Plaintiff filed this action is May 30, 2012. On April 10, 2013, Defendant filed for a trial de novo. The time beginning on the date four years and six months after the plaintiff has filed the action (i.e., May 30, 2012) ending on the date on which a request for trial de novo following arbitration is excluded in computing the five-year period. (CCP § 1141.17.) Therefore, the time period from May 30, 2012 to April 10, 2013 is excluded. This is 315 days. Three hundred fifteen days from April 10, 2013 is February 19, 2014. Therefore, the trial date currently set for August 26, 2013 is prior to the end of the five year period (February 19, 2014). Defendant's reliance on Howard v. Thrifty Drug & Discount Stores (1995) 10 Cal.4th 424 is not dispositive. In Howard, "the five-year statute continued to run between the time the trial request was made and the time some six months later when plaintiff made a motion to specially set the case for trial." (Howard v. Thrifty Drug & Discount Stores (1995) 10 Cal.4th 424, 435.) The five year period in the instant action has not lapsed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Req And Req For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Tuesday, July 30, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Notice of Request and Request for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is not satisfied that the parties have fully met their obligation to meet and confer about the motions. Accordingly, the parties' counsel are directed to confer prior to the hearing (perhaps at 8:00 AM on the day of the hearing) and attempt to simplify and condense the matters on which they are not in agreement. Counsel should be prepared to present the court with the results of their negotiations. A ruling will be deferred until this additional meet and confer requirement has been fulfilled. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Deft'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 30, 2013, Line 24, PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS BLASGEN Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Deft'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration The Petition to Compel Mediation and Arbitration is denied. The Operating Agreement for Refractive Dialer, LLC, on which the petition is based, does not contain a binding agreement to arbitrate. (Decl. of Joshua Tillman In Support of Petition to Compel Mediation and Arbitration and Stay Action, filed May 1, 2013, 4, Ex. A.) Section VII (4) is entitled "Mediation and Arbitration of Disputes Among Members." The operative language states that "Any party may commence arbitration of the dispute by sending a written request for arbitration to all other parties to the dispute. The request shall state the nature of the dispute to be resolved by arbitration, and, if all parties to the dispute agree to arbitration, arbitration shall be commenced as soon as practical after such parties receive a copy of the written request." (Id. Section VII (4) (emphasis added).) The Court has no authority to compel mediation. Requests for attorneys fees are denied. The Court confirms that the previous order announced in Court on June 5, 2013 granting the petition to compel arbitration is vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Julie Walker'S Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part Deft Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 30, 2013, Line 23, Plaintiff Julie Walker'S Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part Deft Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpoena Granted in its entirety as to the subpoenas directed to Plaintiff's former employers, and granted, in part as to the medical subpoenas. PG&E's arguments in opposition lack legal authority and factual justification. The medical subpoenas must be limited to the condition at issue, that is, Ms. Walker's workplace emotional distress. (Britt v. Sup. Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.) The Court will sign the proposed protective order submitted by Plaintiff, with the exception of Attachment B (or any reference to attachment B in the protective order). It creates confusion to have the acknowledgment and agreement restate or paraphrase the terms of the Court order to which the signatory is bound. Defendant failed to meet and confer with Plaintiff on the subject of this motion, including not informing Plaintiff until after the motion was filed that it was not going to proceed with service of subpoenas on former employees. (See Civil Procedure Code § 2023.020.) In addition, the opposition to the motion was without substantial justification. (See Civil Procedure Code § 2023.010(h).) On either or both of these grounds, a monetary sanction of $1,050.00 (representing Plaintiff's attorneys fees) is awarded to Ms. Walker and against PG&E, and is to be paid within 30 days. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY CAUWELS et al VS. DANA JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For An Order To Transfer And Consolidate Cases
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 30, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY CAUWELS, LESLIE CAUWELS Ntc Of Motion For An Order To Transfer And Consolidate Cases Granted. The ends of justice are promoted by transferring the Contra Costa action (Case MSC13-00783) to this Court and consolidating it with this action. The actions are not complex. Common questions of fact and law predominate and are significant to the litigation. DTG Defendants agree that transfer and consolidate will promote the ends of justice consistent with the standards set forth in CCP § 404.1. The only disputed issue is where the matter should be transferred. Counsel for plaintiffs in this case and in the Contra Costa action stipulate that the Contra Costa action should be transferred to San Francisco. The convenience of the parties is promoted in San Francisco. Defendant DTG maintains offices and conducts business in San Francisco, one of its employee defendants resides here, and another in San Mateo County. It is not inconvenient for defense counsel, with offices in Oakland, to come to San Francisco for proceedings in this case. As for witnesses, some are in San Francisco and others are in the East Bay, but there is no significant reason to transfer the case to Contra Costa on that account given the proximity of the two counties. Furthermore, counsel for Plaintiff in the Contra Costa action (City of Pinole) has stipulated to having its case heard in San Francisco, which is a strong indication that witnesses in that case will not be inconvenienced by the proposed transfer which, taking into account all factors, promotes the interests of justice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
